$schema: RateGuard cross-language conformance vectors — token bucket.
description: One shared oracle for the token-bucket admission decision (allowed, remaining, retry_after_ms), replayed by all 3 SDKs (Go, Node.js, Python) against their own Store.Increment. Proves real behavioral parity, not just matching test counts. See AGENTS.md rule 1: 'same algorithm, same API, same behavior.'
formula: tokens = min(burst, tokens + elapsed_seconds * rps); allow if tokens >= n, consume n; idle bucket (>600000ms since last access) resets to a full burst before refilling. On deny: retry_after_ms = max(1000, ceil(deficit_seconds) * 1000) — always a whole-second multiple, never a bare millisecond value.

steps.note: ask for 10 with only 6 left — deny, no consumption
consume exactly the 6 remaining — allow, bucket now empty
partial refill: 0.3s * 10rps = 3 tokens, not capped by burst
refill would exceed burst — clamps to burst before consuming
idle >10min triggers the reset-to-full branch, not just a large refill
drain the remaining 9 tokens exactly — allow, bucket now empty
deficit of 15 tokens at 10rps = 1.5s — must ceil to 2 whole seconds (2000ms), not floor/truncate to 1500ms; this is the case that previously diverged across SDKs
